topic: China considers restricting foreign access to advanced AI models
headline: CHINA EYES RESTRICTIVE AI ACCESS, WHILE US BLOCKS CHINA FROM ADVANCED CHIPS IN TENSE TECH SHOWDOWN
summary: China is considering restricting foreign access to advanced AI models, while the US has already blocked sales of certain AI chips to China. China's tech supply chain is adapting to these restrictions. The Chinese government is also promoting the use of AI, with state backing and support from leaders like President Xi Jinping.

summary_sources.title: The rapid embrace of AI in China, its biggest testing ground, may shape how AI is used globally, ETCIO
Biden administration proposes new rules on exporting AI chips | AP News
US blocks sales of some AI chips to China as tech crackdown intensifies | China | The Guardian
Six months after DeepSeek’s breakthrough, China speeds on with AI | Mint

sections.paragraph: The Biden administration has implemented export controls on cutting-edge semiconductors used for AI systems, aiming to counter China's military ambitions. This move has alarmed China, with President Xi Jinping accusing the US of orchestrating a campaign to contain his country. The new rule restricts not just US companies but any manufacturer using US software or technology, effectively giving the US control over chips worldwide. The proposed framework has also raised concerns among chip industry executives and officials from the European Union, as it would affect 120 countries and limit access to chips needed for AI data centers and products. Meanwhile, China's exports have jumped 27% in June, driven by strong demand for AI-related items, but the country still faces risks and challenges due to rising trade barriers. The US policy may have unintended consequences, such as emboldening China to attack Taiwan or prompting other countries to seek technological self-sufficiency, and its impact on geopolitics remains to be seen.

sources.title: How the U.S. stumbled into using chips as a weapon against China | Mint
Biden administration proposes new rules on exporting AI chips | AP News
China's exports jump 27% in June on strong demand for AI

sections.paragraph: China is rapidly advancing in the field of artificial intelligence, with the country becoming a significant testing ground for AI technologies. The release of DeepSeek's V4 AI model, which is supported in part by Chinese tech giant Huawei's computer chips, has reduced China's dependence on top US chipmakers such as Nvidia. This development, along with state backing from President Xi Jinping, has fueled the adoption of AI in various industries, with experts focusing on identifying applications for specific sectors. The use of AI is spreading rapidly, with many experts arguing that rapid adoption is key, and events such as the installation of AI assistants have drawn large crowds. China's AI boom is also attracting investors, with the suburb of Liangzhu in Hangzhou emerging as a hub for AI startups, representing the country's future in the field. However, export controls on tools have slowed China's chipmaking capabilities, which could potentially impact its AI ambitions, although some experts believe this could ultimately fuel China's advancements.

sources.title: The rapid embrace of AI in China, its biggest testing ground, may shape how AI is used globally, ETCIO
Six months after DeepSeek’s breakthrough, China speeds on with AI | Mint

sections.paragraph: The economics of AI is undergoing a significant shift, with the rise of China's DeepSeek potentially altering the global landscape. DeepSeek's success has been achieved at a fraction of the cost of its US rivals, and its impact has been substantial, with $1tn wiped off the value of US tech stocks. The US had previously restricted the export of high-tech components to China, which led to DeepSeek's development using lower-end technology. Meanwhile, the US government has lifted restrictions on the export of powerful AI models, such as Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5, which were previously blocked on national security grounds. This move is expected to restore access to these models globally. The changing economic reality may lead to a tightening of belts and lowered valuation expectations for AI companies, as they explore new ways to monetize their technologies.

sources.title: DeepSeek shows AI's centre of power could shift away from US
